channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: California House race flips to narrow lead for Dem amid weekslong vote count
item.description: Democrat Derek Tran has taken the lead over GOP Rep. Michelle Steel in California's 45th Congressional District with 6% of the vote still uncounted two weeks after the election.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles passes sanctuary city ordinance in wake of Trump election victory
item.description: Los Angeles voted to become a sanctuary city, as the Trump administration promises to carry out mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans signal support for proposal to ban bathroom access for 1st transgender member
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Speaker Mike Johnson signaled support Tuesday for a Republican effort to ban Democrat Sarah McBride — the first transgender person to be elected to Congress — from using women’s restrooms in the Capitol once she's sworn into office next year.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors oppose tossing Trump’s hush money conviction but are open to sentencing delay
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York prosecutors oppose any effort to dismiss President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction, but they expressed openness Tuesday to delaying sentencing until after his second term.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Dr. Oz to lead Medicare and Medicaid
item.description: In announcing his pick, Trump said Oz will work closely with RFK, Jr. “to take on the illness industrial complex.” Oz, a celebrity physician, ran unsuccessfully for the U.S. Senate in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Diane Webber and Carmel Wroth, MPR News

item.title: GOP senators divided on release of Gaetz ethics report as Trump pressures them to move quickly
item.description: As President-elect Donald Trump digs in on his pick of Matt Gaetz for attorney general, Republican senators are divided over how much information they will demand to move his confirmation — and how much to push back on Trump as he demands that they quickly rubber stamp his Cabinet once he takes offi
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump appoints Dr. Oz to key HHS position in new administration
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Tuesday that Dr. Mehmet Oz will be his nominee for Administrator to the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dems sue PA election board over uncounted provisional ballots amid Senate recount in Casey loss
item.description: The lawsuit by the DSCC centers on dozens of provisional ballots in Bucks County, Pennsylvania, that lacked a 'secrecy envelope'
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vance will introduce Gaetz, Hegseth to senators this week
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance will make the rounds with GOP senators later this week to introduce them to former-Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) and Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s picks to head the Justice and Defense departments.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bragg case 'effectively over' in 'major victory,' Trump officials say
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Trump officials told Fox News Digital that Manhattan DA Alvin Bragg’s case is “effectively over," after he requested a stay until 2029, marking a “major victory" for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rick Scott outlines consensus for 'dramatic change' to Senate operation in post-McConnell era
item.description: Sen. Rick Scott detailed a number of areas in which Republicans overwhelmingly want change ahead of the new Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Manhattan DA opposes Trump’s bid to dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: Manhattan prosecutors on Tuesday said they will oppose President-elect Trump’s demand to dismiss his New York criminal conviction following the election, instead floating the idea the judge could freeze the case until Trump leaves office.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's hush money case should be paused, not dismissed: Prosecutors
item.description: Trump was convicted in May of falsifying business records to cover up a $130,000 payment his former lawyer Michael Cohen made to porn star Stormy Daniels.
item.dc:creator: Luc Cohen / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: GOP senators reject replacing FBI background checks for Trump nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans are rejecting a proposal floated by some advisers to President-elect Trump to take the job of conducting background checks for high-level nominees away from the FBI and give it to private investigators.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: The order is a win for McCormick and a loss for Casey as the campaigns prepare for a statewide recount and press counties for favorable ballot-counting decisions while election workers are sorting through thousands of provisional ballots.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Matt Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Leppard said the House Ethics Committee and his clients went one by one through a series of Venmo transactions.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX launches 'Starship' rocket
item.description: Leading up to the Nov. 5 election, SpaceX CEO Elon Musk showed support for Trump while on the campaign trail.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Yañez, NewsNation

item.title: All eyes on House Ethics Committee amid controversy over Gaetz report
item.description: All eyes will be locked on the House Ethics Committee this week as it weighs whether to release its report into Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio colleges could be banned from asking student pronouns on applications
item.description: Ohio's public universities could be banned from asking prospective students their preferred pronouns on applications.
item.dc:creator: David Rees, NewsNation

item.title: Accused of violating worker rights, SpaceX and Amazon go after labor board
item.description: SpaceX and Amazon are asking the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals to find the National Labor Relations Board unconstitutional. The federal agency is tasked with enforcing workers' right to organize.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: Biden and Xi take a first step to limit AI and nuclear decisions at their last meeting
item.description: The leaders agreed that humans should control decisions to use nuclear weapons — not AI. And they talked about the importance of stability during the transition to a new U.S. administration.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Trump names fossil fuel executive Chris Wright as energy secretary
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has selected Chris Wright, a campaign donor and fossil fuel executive, to serve as Energy secretary after his second administration begins in January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Florida congressional leaders demand answers from FEMA political scandal
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) - Republican and Democratic leaders on Capitol Hill are preparing for a hearing next week with FEMA administrator Deanne Criswell, regarding alleged political discrimination after hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump picks Karoline Leavitt to be youngest White House press secretary in history
item.description: Leavitt will be the youngest person to ever hold the job and is expected to deliver press briefings based on Trump’s statement late Friday that he has “utmost confidence she will excel at the podium.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump-DeSantis complicated relationship could impact Senate pick: political expert
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis has a big decision to make if Sen. Marco Rubio is confirmed as the next Secretary of State.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What’s ahead for abortion access in Minnesota under a Trump administration
item.description: While detailed plans have yet to emerge, health and legal experts, practitioners and advocates in Minnesota are closely monitoring the new administration for hints about what federal changes to abortion could mean for the state.
item.dc:creator: Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: RFK Jr. wants to 'Make America Healthy Again.' He could face a lot of pushback
item.description: RFK Jr. wants to tackle chronic disease. Despite controversial views on vaccines, his focus on healthy food and taking on special interests may find broad support — and face political headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone and Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: Abbott to Texas universities: Freeze tuition
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott (R) announced Wednesday that he has sent letters to all public universities in the state saying he would not support tuition raises over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
